rows.Tax Treatment: Qualified div rate (0/15/20%); LT cap gains
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Voting rights. Last in line at liquidation. No maturity.
Usually NO voting rights. Senior to common in liquidation. Acts like a bond — price moves inverse to rates.

rows.Risk Level: Moderate to High (currency + foreign country risk)
rows.Tax Treatment: Qualified if treaty country; foreign tax credit possible
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: American Depositary Receipt. US dollars buy foreign companies. Currency risk is the trap answer.

rows.Tax Treatment: Cap gain/loss when exercised or sold
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Short life (30-45 days). Lets existing shareholders buy NEW shares BELOW market. Avoid dilution.
Long-term (years). Issued ABOVE market as 'sweetener' attached to bonds. Speculative.

rows.Tax Treatment: Ordinary income on interest; cap gain/loss on sale
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Investment grade = BBB-/Baa3+. Senior to all stockholders in liquidation.

rows.Tax Treatment: Ordinary on interest; cap gain on conversion
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Converts to common at fixed ratio. Pays LOWER coupon for conversion privilege. Acts like stock when in-the-money.

rows.Tax Treatment: Federally tax-EXEMPT; state-exempt if in-state issuer
rows.Key Reg / Act: MSRB rules (FINRA enforces); '33/'34 Acts mostly exempt
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Backed by full faith, credit, AND TAXING POWER. Safer than revenue bonds.
Backed ONLY by project revenue, NOT taxing power. Riskier than GO. Examples: toll roads, water utilities.

rows.Risk Level: Lowest (full faith and credit of US)
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Maturity ≤ 1 year (4, 8, 13, 17, 26, 52 weeks). Bought at discount, mature at par.
Maturity 2-10 years. Quoted in 32nds (e.g., 99.16 = 99 + 16/32).
Maturity 20-30 years. Most price-sensitive bonds to rate changes due to long duration.

rows.Tax Treatment: Fed tax on interest AND yearly inflation adjustment ('phantom income')
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Principal adjusts with CPI. Real return guaranteed. Phantom income trap on inflation accretion.

rows.Tax Treatment: Fed tax accreted yearly (phantom income); state-EXEMPT
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Zero-coupon Treasuries. Most price-sensitive of all bonds. Yearly phantom income tax.
Pass-through securities. GNMA = FULL govt guarantee. FNMA/FHLMC = implied/GSE backing.
Collateralized Mortgage Obligations. Tranches: PAC, TAC, Companion, Z. Sliced by prepayment risk.

rows.Tax Treatment: Distributions taxed in year received, even if reinvested
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Forward-priced at NAV at 4:00 PM ET close. A=front load, B=back/CDSC, C=level (1%), I=institutional.
Trade like stocks intraday. Lower expenses than mutual funds. Tax-efficient via in-kind creation/redemption.
FIXED unmanaged portfolio. Self-liquidates at termination date. No active management = lower cost.
FIXED share count. Trades at premium/discount to NAV. Can use leverage (unlike mutual funds).
MUST pay 90% of income out. Equity REIT = owns properties. Mortgage REIT = owns mortgage debt.
Direct Participation Programs. Real estate, oil & gas, equipment leasing. Limited partner liability capped at investment.

rows.Key Reg / Act: Reg D usually; Investment Advisers Act for manager
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Accredited investors / QIBs only. '2 and 20' fee structure. Lock-up periods. Manager registers as IA.

rows.Income Type: Tax-deferred growth; ordinary income at distribution
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Separate account holds sub-accounts. Need BOTH securities AND insurance license to sell.

rows.Tax Treatment: Tax-deferred growth; ordinary income on withdrawal
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: NOT a security. No securities license needed. Insurance company guarantees the rate.

rows.Tax Treatment: Cap gain/loss; LEAPS can be long-term
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Standard contract = 100 shares. Expires 3rd Friday at 11:59 PM ET. OCC is buyer to every seller, seller to every buyer.
Cash-settled in dollars (no shares delivered). Index × multiplier. Broad-based gets 60/40 tax.
Negotiable CDs ($100K+) trade in secondary market. FDIC-insured up to $250K per depositor per bank.
Short-term unsecured corp debt. Exempt from registration if MATURITY ≤ 270 DAYS.
Time draft accepted (guaranteed) by a bank. Used for international trade financing.

rows.Income Type: Yield from repurchase price minus sale price
rows.Notes / Mnemonics: Repurchase agreement. Sale + commitment to buy back. Overnight or term. Backed by govt collateral.

rows.Nickname: The Paper Act / Truth in Securities
rows.Key Provisions: Registration of new issues. Prospectus required. Cooling-off period (20 days min). Civil liability for misstatements.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Confused with 1934 Act. Remember: 1933 = NEW issues, 1934 = TRADING.

rows.Key Provisions: Created the SEC. Regulates B/Ds, exchanges, insider trading. Section 16 reports. Proxy rules.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Trick: 1934 Act CREATED the SEC, not the 1933 Act.

rows.Key Provisions: Classifies investment companies: Face-amount, UIT, Management. Registration, disclosure, 12b-1 fees.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Regulates MUTUAL FUNDS and other inv. companies. NOT investment advisers (separate '40 Act).

rows.Key Provisions: Defines IA. SEC registration if AUM ≥ $100M; state if less. Fiduciary duty to clients.
rows.Common Exam Trap: AUM threshold matters: $100M+ = SEC; under $100M = state.

rows.Key Provisions: Requires independent trustee for corporate bond issues over $50M. Trust indenture filed with SEC.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Applies to CORPORATE bonds only. Munis and Treasuries are EXEMPT.

rows.Act / Rule: Insider Trading & Securities Fraud Enforcement Act 1988
rows.Key Provisions: Treble damages (3x profit gained or loss avoided). Controlling person liability. Bounty payments to informants.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Penalties = up to 3x the profit/loss. Plus criminal penalties separately.

rows.Key Provisions: CEO/CFO must certify financials. PCAOB created to oversee auditors. Whistleblower protections.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Created in response to Enron/WorldCom. PCAOB oversees AUDITORS not B/Ds.

rows.Key Provisions: Created CFPB. Volcker Rule. SEC whistleblower bounties. Required hedge fund advisers to register.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Response to 2008 crisis. Major restructuring of financial regulation.

rows.Key Provisions: Crowdfunding (Reg CF). Reg A+. Loosened IPO rules for EGCs. Allowed general solicitation under Reg D 506(c).
rows.Common Exam Trap: Created Reg A+ (Tier 2: $75M cap) and equity crowdfunding under Reg CF.

rows.Key Provisions: Created SIPC. Insures customer assets when broker-dealer fails. $500K total / $250K cash.
rows.Common Exam Trap: NOT FDIC. Covers MISSING securities/cash — does NOT cover loss of market value.

rows.Key Provisions: Customer Identification Program (CIP). CTRs for cash $10K+. SARs for suspicion $5K+. FinCEN oversight.
rows.Common Exam Trap: CTR = factual ($10K cash). SAR = suspicion ($5K) and CONFIDENTIAL (never tell customer).

rows.Key Provisions: Holding period: 6 mo (reporting company) / 12 mo (non-reporting). Volume limits for affiliates.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Affiliates have ONGOING volume restrictions. Non-affiliates just have the hold period.

rows.Key Provisions: Allows QIBs to trade unregistered securities freely among themselves. No public offering needed.
rows.Common Exam Trap: QIB = $100M+ in invested assets. Used heavily in private placements.

rows.Key Provisions: Exempt from federal registration if offered and sold ONLY within one state. 147A allows internet under conditions.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Pure local offerings. State Blue Sky laws still apply.

rows.Key Provisions: Tier 1: $20M/12mo, state review required. Tier 2: $75M/12mo, no state review (preempted), ongoing reports.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Tier 2 preempts state Blue Sky review. Tier 2 has a 10%-of-income/net-worth cap for non-accredited.

rows.Key Provisions: 504: $10M/12mo cap. 506(b): unlimited $, up to 35 non-accredited, NO solicitation. 506(c): unlimited $, accredited ONLY, solicitation OK.
rows.Common Exam Trap: 506(b) = no solicitation. 506(c) = solicitation OK but verification REQUIRED.

rows.Key Provisions: Securities sold offshore to non-US persons are exempt from US registration.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Tested as the 'foreign sales exemption'.

rows.Key Provisions: 50% initial margin requirement. Set by the Federal Reserve Board.
rows.Common Exam Trap: FED sets Reg T (not SEC/FINRA). 50% INITIAL only — maintenance is FINRA's 25%/30%.

rows.Key Provisions: Sets margin requirements for BANKS lending against securities collateral.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Reg T = broker-dealers. Reg U = banks. Often confused on exam.

rows.Key Provisions: Restricts trading by underwriters/participants during distribution to prevent price manipulation.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Quiet period for analysts/underwriters during new issue distribution.

rows.Key Provisions: 'Locate and deliver' requirement. Marking orders short/short-exempt. Closeout requirements.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Locate requirement: B/D must reasonably believe shares can be borrowed BEFORE accepting short order.

rows.Key Provisions: Replaces suitability for retail. Best interest standard. Disclosure obligations. Conflict-of-interest management.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Effective June 2020. Replaced FINRA suitability for retail recommendations.

rows.Key Provisions: 2-page plain-English summary delivered to retail investors. Services, fees, conflicts, disciplinary history.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Required at start of relationship and after material updates.

rows.Key Provisions: Must obtain Name, DOB, Address, ID number (SSN/EIN/passport). Verify identity. Beneficial ownership rules added 2018.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Required at account OPENING — not later. Don't confuse with KYC (broader concept).

rows.What It Is: Regulation T initial margin requirement set by the Federal Reserve
rows.Memory Hook: Fifty for the Fed (Reg T = Fed)

rows.What It Is: FINRA minimum maintenance margin for LONG positions
rows.Memory Hook: Long is lower (25), short is higher (30)

rows.What It Is: FINRA minimum maintenance margin for SHORT positions
Pattern Day Trader minimum equity (4+ day trades in 5 biz days)
rows.Memory Hook: Four trades in five days → need $25K

rows.What It Is: Minimum cooling-off period after S-1 filing
Quiet period after IPO (NYSE/AMEX listed)
rows.Memory Hook: Twenty-five for the silence after listing

rows.What It Is: Quiet period after IPO for non-listed (OTC)
Cross 5% beneficial ownership → file Sch 13D within 10 days
Insiders (officers/directors/10%+ holders) file Form 4 after transactions
Accredited investor: income individual / income joint / net worth (ex primary residence)
QIB (Qualified Institutional Buyer) minimum invested assets
Reg A Tier 1 maximum offering in 12 months
rows.Memory Hook: Tier 1 = lower amount, has state review

rows.What It Is: Reg A Tier 2 maximum offering in 12 months
Reg D Rule 504 maximum in 12 months
Restricted stock holding period: reporting co. / non-reporting
2026 IRA limits: $7,500 base + $1,100 catch-up at 50+.
2026 401(k) limits: $24,500 base + $8,000 catch-up at 50+. Super catch-up for ages 60-63: $11,250.
Required Minimum Distribution starting age (SECURE Act 2.0)
Age before which 10% early-withdrawal penalty applies
rows.Memory Hook: Half past 59 to dodge the penalty

rows.What It Is: CTR — Currency Transaction Report (CASH transactions)
SAR — Suspicious Activity Report threshold
12b-1 max: distribution fee (0.75%) + service fee (0.25%) = 1.00% total
No-load definition — total 12b-1 must be ≤ 0.25%
Forward pricing — NAV calculated once daily at market close
Standard equity options expiration
Annual gift exclusion per donor per recipient. Anchors 529 superfunding.
Sell at loss, buy back within 30 days BEFORE OR AFTER = disallowed loss
rows.Memory Hook: 30 each side of trade = 61-day total window

rows.What It Is: FINRA 5% policy GUIDELINE on B/D markups/markdowns (NOT a hard rule)
Trust Indenture Act applies to corporate bond issues over $50M
SIPC max coverage per customer: total / cash sub-limit
FDIC bank deposit insurance per depositor per insured bank
Standard settlement for stocks, bonds, ETFs, options (May 2024+)
Same-day settlement for Treasuries and options (technically)

rows.Tax Treatment: Pre-tax contributions; ordinary income at withdrawal
rows.Withdrawal Rules: 59½ minimum (10% penalty before); RMDs starting age 73
rows.Key Distinction: Tax deduction NOW, taxes LATER
rows.Suitability Notes: For clients expecting LOWER tax bracket in retirement

rows.Contribution Limit: Same $ limit as Traditional, but income phase-outs apply
rows.Withdrawal Rules: Contributions anytime tax/penalty-free; earnings need 59½ AND 5-year rule
rows.Key Distinction: Pay NOW, FREE forever. NO RMDs during owner lifetime.
rows.Suitability Notes: For clients expecting HIGHER tax bracket in retirement; younger savers

rows.Tax Treatment: Standard tax; gains/losses on Schedule D
rows.Key Distinction: Most basic account — cash settles all trades
rows.Suitability Notes: Default account for general investing

rows.Tax Treatment: Each owner taxed on their pro-rata share
rows.Key Distinction: Owners can have UNEQUAL shares. Share passes via WILL, not survivor.
rows.Suitability Notes: Use when ownership shares are unequal (business partners)

rows.Account Type: Joint Tenants with Right of Survivorship (JTWROS)
rows.Tax Treatment: Each taxed on share; full step-up on death of one
rows.Key Distinction: Equal shares. Passes AUTOMATICALLY to survivor. Avoids probate.
rows.Suitability Notes: Default for married couples in most states

rows.Tax Treatment: Same as JTWROS but with creditor protection
rows.Key Distinction: Like JTWROS + creditor protection. Available only in some states.
rows.Suitability Notes: Most protective from individual creditors. Spouses only.

rows.Tax Treatment: Interest deductible against investment income (margin interest)
rows.Withdrawal Rules: Any time (subject to margin call rules)
rows.Key Distinction: Borrows on collateral. Reg T 50% initial, FINRA 25%/30% maintenance.
rows.Suitability Notes: NEVER for retirement accounts. Need account agreement + signed hypothecation.

rows.Key Distinction: B/D needs WRITTEN authorization before trading without prior approval.
rows.Suitability Notes: Prior written authorization required. Higher supervisory standard.

rows.Tax Treatment: Kiddie tax (some at parent's marginal rate)
rows.Contribution Limit: No limit (but watch annual gift exclusion)
rows.Withdrawal Rules: Custodian controls until age of majority (18-25 by state)
rows.Key Distinction: Irrevocable gift to minor. Wider asset range than UGMA.
rows.Suitability Notes: Education savings, gifting strategy. Asset hurts financial aid eligibility.

rows.Withdrawal Rules: Custodian controls until age of majority (state-dependent)
rows.Key Distinction: MORE RESTRICTED asset types than UTMA (older, narrower).
rows.Suitability Notes: Largely replaced by UTMA in most states

rows.Tax Treatment: Tax-deferred growth; tax-FREE for qualified ed expenses (K-12 + college)
rows.Key Distinction: Owner KEEPS control. Beneficiary can be changed within family.
rows.Suitability Notes: Best general education savings vehicle. State tax deduction in many states.

rows.Withdrawal Rules: Used for ed expenses; reverts at age 30
rows.Key Distinction: K-12 expenses qualify (broader than 529 originally). Income phase-outs apply.
rows.Suitability Notes: Lower limit than 529 but historically more flexible

rows.Tax Treatment: Pre-tax employer contributions; ordinary income at withdrawal
rows.Withdrawal Rules: Same as Traditional IRA — 59½, RMDs at 73
rows.Key Distinction: EMPLOYER-funded only. No employee contributions. Easy to administer.
rows.Suitability Notes: Best for self-employed with high income looking for simple plan

rows.Tax Treatment: Pre-tax employee + employer; ordinary income at withdrawal
rows.Withdrawal Rules: Same as Traditional IRA, PLUS 2-year withdrawal restriction in early years
rows.Key Distinction: Mandatory employer match (up to 3%) or 2% non-elective contribution
rows.Suitability Notes: For small employers wanting cheaper alternative to 401(k)

rows.Tax Treatment: Pre-tax employee + employer; ordinary income at withdrawal
rows.Withdrawal Rules: 59½ for penalty-free; RMDs at 73; in-service withdrawals limited
rows.Key Distinction: Employer-sponsored. Can include employer match. Self-directed within plan options.
rows.Suitability Notes: Most common workplace retirement plan

rows.Withdrawal Rules: 59½ + 5-year rule for tax-free; RMDs at 73 (unlike Roth IRA)
rows.Key Distinction: NO income phase-outs (unlike Roth IRA). Can split with Traditional 401(k).
rows.Suitability Notes: Best for high earners locked out of Roth IRA

rows.Contribution Limit: Employee deferral + employer contribution up to $69K (2024)
rows.Key Distinction: Combines employee deferral + employer contribution from same person.
rows.Suitability Notes: Highest contribution potential for sole proprietors

rows.Tax Treatment: Pre-tax employee + employer; ordinary income at withdrawal
rows.Key Distinction: For 501(c)(3) orgs, schools, hospitals, churches.
rows.Suitability Notes: Like 401(k) for the non-profit sector

rows.Withdrawal Rules: NO 10% early-withdrawal penalty if separated from service (unique!)
rows.Key Distinction: Govt employees can max BOTH 457 + 403/401 in same year.
rows.Suitability Notes: Unique: no early-withdrawal penalty. Stacks with 403(b)/401(k).

rows.Key Distinction: Owner has UNLIMITED personal liability
rows.Suitability Notes: Simplest business form, but no liability protection

rows.Key Distinction: GP = unlimited liability. LP = limited to investment.
rows.Suitability Notes: Liability profile differs sharply between GP and LP

rows.Tax Treatment: Default pass-through; can elect S-corp or C-corp
rows.Key Distinction: Members get LIMITED liability. Flexible tax election.
rows.Suitability Notes: Most flexible business structure

rows.Tax Treatment: C-corp: double-taxed. S-corp: pass-through (max 100 US shareholders).
rows.Key Distinction: Limited liability. S-corp restricted to ≤100 US-person shareholders.
rows.Suitability Notes: Liability protection at cost of complexity

rows.Tax Treatment: Revocable: grantor taxed. Irrevocable: trust taxed.
rows.Key Distinction: Revocable CAN be changed. Irrevocable CANNOT.
rows.Suitability Notes: Estate planning. Irrevocable removes assets from grantor's estate.

rows.Key Distinction: Transfer on Death / Payable on Death. Avoids probate.
rows.Suitability Notes: Simple probate bypass. Doesn't replace a full estate plan.

rows.When to Use: Strong bullish view; leverage with capped downside
rows.Notes: Right (not obligation) to BUY 100 shares at strike

rows.When to Use: Hedge long stock; or speculate on decline
rows.Notes: Right to SELL 100 shares at strike

rows.When to Use: Income on stock you expect to stay below strike
rows.Notes: HIGHEST-risk basic strategy. Big margin required.

rows.When to Use: Income; willing to buy stock at lower price if assigned
rows.Notes: Less risky than naked call (loss is capped by stock at $0)

rows.When to Use: Generate income on long stock; willing to sell at strike
rows.Notes: Caps upside at strike + premium. Most common income strategy.

rows.When to Use: Insurance on long stock against decline
rows.Notes: Married = bought together at same time. Protective = added later.

rows.Max Gain: Unlimited up (call side); substantial down (put side)
rows.When to Use: Volatility play: earnings, FDA decisions, regulatory rulings
rows.Notes: SAME strike + SAME expiration. Buy 1 call + 1 put.

rows.When to Use: Income; expecting price to stay still
rows.Notes: Very high risk. Significant margin. Profit zone is narrow.

rows.Outlook: Big move expected; cheaper than straddle
rows.Breakeven: Lower strike - Total Prem; Higher strike + Total Prem
rows.When to Use: Volatility play with lower entry cost than straddle
rows.Notes: Different OTM strikes; same expiration. Wider breakevens than straddle.

rows.Outlook: Low volatility; wider profit zone than short straddle
rows.Breakeven: Lower strike - Prems; Higher strike + Prems
rows.When to Use: Income strategy with wider profit range
rows.Notes: High risk but less than short straddle. Different OTM strikes.

rows.Max Gain: High Strike - Low Strike - Net Debit
rows.When to Use: Bullish but limited capital; want capped risk
rows.Notes: BUY lower call, SELL higher call. Debit spread.

rows.Max Loss: High Strike - Low Strike - Net Credit
rows.When to Use: Bearish income strategy with capped risk
rows.Notes: SELL lower call, BUY higher call. Credit spread.

rows.Max Loss: High Strike - Low Strike - Net Credit
rows.When to Use: Bullish income strategy with capped risk
rows.Notes: SELL higher put, BUY lower put. Credit spread.

rows.Max Gain: High Strike - Low Strike - Net Debit
rows.When to Use: Bearish with limited capital and capped risk
rows.Notes: BUY higher put, SELL lower put. Debit spread.
Sells OTM call spread + OTM put spread. Four legs.

rows.Outlook: Neutral; price expected near middle strike at expiration
rows.Max Gain: Middle Strike - Lower Strike - Net Debit
rows.Breakeven: Lower Strike + Debit / Higher Strike - Debit
rows.When to Use: Very specific price target at expiration
rows.Notes: Low cost, low reward, low risk. Sweet spot at center strike.

rows.Max Gain: Call Strike - Stock Cost + Net Credit (or - Debit)
rows.Max Loss: Stock Cost - Put Strike - Net Credit (or + Debit)
rows.When to Use: Hedge long stock cheaply (sell call to fund put)
rows.Notes: Buy protective put + Sell covered call. Often near-zero cost.

rows.Plain English: What % return you'd earn at today's price
Just the stated coupon rate — never changes
Total return if held to maturity, including coupon AND price gain/loss
rows.Example: Key concept: YTM > CY when bond at DISCOUNT; YTM < CY when at PREMIUM

rows.Formula: Same as YTM but use call price + years to call
rows.Plain English: Total return if bond is called at first call date
rows.Example: For premium callable bonds, YTC is the more conservative (lower) yield to quote

rows.Plain English: What you actually keep after federal tax on a corporate bond
What a taxable bond would need to yield to match a tax-free muni
Per-share value of the fund — calculated once daily at 4 PM ET
Public Offering Price — what investor pays (NAV + load)
The actual % markup the customer pays as a load
Stock price at expiration where long call breaks even
rows.Example: $50 strike call bought at $2 premium → BE = $52

rows.Plain English: Stock price at expiration where long put breaks even
rows.Example: $50 strike put bought at $2 premium → BE = $48

rows.Plain English: What the option is worth if exercised right now — never negative
rows.Example: Stock $52, call strike $50 → $2 intrinsic. Stock $48 → $0 intrinsic.

rows.Plain English: Profit if exercised today — never negative
rows.Example: Stock $48, put strike $50 → $2 intrinsic. Stock $52 → $0 intrinsic.

rows.Plain English: The 'speculative' portion of premium — decays to zero at expiration
Stock price at which long position triggers maintenance call (25%)
rows.Example: Bought $20K stock with $10K loan → call at $10K ÷ 0.75 = $13,333

rows.Plain English: Your TRUE purchasing-power return after inflation
Income return on a stock at current price
Combined return from income + price appreciation
rows.Example: Stock: $5 gain + $2 div on $50 cost basis → 14% total return
Bought at $50, sold at $65 → $15 capital gain

rows.Common Exam Trap: The SEC was created by the 1934 Act, NOT the 1933 Act.

rows.Key Powers: Writes/enforces conduct rules, administers Series exams, arbitration
rows.Common Exam Trap: NOT a government agency. Reports up to the SEC.

rows.Key Powers: Writes rules for muni dealers and advisers; FINRA enforces on B/Ds
rows.Common Exam Trap: MSRB WRITES rules but does NOT enforce on B/Ds — FINRA does.

rows.Key Powers: Insures $500K total / $250K cash when B/D fails
rows.Common Exam Trap: NOT a govt agency. NOT FDIC. Does NOT cover loss of market value.

rows.Key Powers: Insures bank deposits up to $250K per depositor per insured bank
rows.Common Exam Trap: FDIC = banks. SIPC = brokerage accounts. They never overlap.

rows.Key Powers: Sets Reg T (50% initial margin); controls money supply via FOMC
rows.Common Exam Trap: Reg T comes from the FED — not SEC or FINRA.

rows.Jurisdiction: Federal finances, debt issuance, tax administration
rows.Key Powers: Issues T-bills, notes, bonds, TIPS. Houses IRS and FinCEN.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Issuer, not regulator. Treasuries are EXEMPT from '33 Act registration.

rows.Key Powers: Receives CTRs ($10K+) and SARs ($5K+); enforces AML rules
rows.Common Exam Trap: Bureau of the Treasury — NOT an independent agency.

rows.Key Powers: Tax treatment of all accounts, securities, and distributions
rows.Common Exam Trap: Sets retirement contribution limits, RMD ages, wash sale rules

rows.Key Powers: Lists securities; sets listing standards; SRO regulated by SEC
rows.Common Exam Trap: Largest US exchange by market cap. Both electronic and physical floor.

rows.Key Powers: Electronic exchange. Multiple tiers (Global Select, Global, Capital).
rows.Common Exam Trap: ALL-ELECTRONIC. No trading floor. Founded as a quotation system.

rows.Key Powers: Lists standardized options. Created listed options market.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Standardized contracts (100 shares, monthly expirations) date from 1973 CBOE launch.

rows.Key Powers: Issues all listed options; guarantees performance; counterparty to every trade
rows.Common Exam Trap: OCC is the buyer to every seller and the seller to every buyer. You never face the original counterparty.

rows.Key Powers: Parent of NSCC, DTC, FICC. Processes virtually all US securities trades.
rows.Common Exam Trap: Holding company for the clearing infrastructure

rows.Key Powers: Nets trades for clearing; acts as central counterparty via novation
rows.Common Exam Trap: Reduces settlement complexity via netting

rows.Key Powers: Sets audit standards for public-company auditors; inspects audit firms
rows.Common Exam Trap: Created in response to Enron/WorldCom. Oversees AUDITORS, not broker-dealers.
Securities = SEC. Futures = CFTC. They're peer agencies, not nested.

rows.Key Powers: Regulates FCMs, intro brokers, CPOs, CTAs
rows.Common Exam Trap: Futures industry SRO. Rarely on SIE but good to know exists.
